questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.

"The District Magistrate ordered the police to work all night and put an end to those riots."

options.text: The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put to end these riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put an end to these riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put an end to their riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night to put an end these riots."

questions.notes: The options are labeled a, b, c, d in the image, mapped to 1, 2, 3, 4 respectively.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.

"The team decided to pull out all the stops for their upcoming presentation."

options.text: Wait patiently for a more favourable time
Give up easily and without thinking much
To do everything to make something successful
